authorization_template.copyable_template:
YES execute P271L controlled production schema deployment
repo=/Users/kelvin/Kelvin-WorkSpace/LotteryNew
main_commit=847262bd1a6efec3fcc3bff879867f71f7555ade
production_db=/Users/kelvin/Kelvin-WorkSpace/LotteryNew/lottery_api/data/lottery_v2.db
expected_pre_apply_sha256=<fresh hash>
backup_path=<outside-repo path>
maintenance_window_confirmed=YES
all_writers_stopped_and_verified=YES
rollback_plan_confirmed=YES
authorization_template.note: This task and this template are NOT current authorization.

destination_requirements.failure_behavior: abort apply; do not proceed without verified backup
destination_requirements.integrity_verification: post-backup sqlite integrity_check + raw hash recorded (under separate authorization)
destination_requirements.wal_handling: checkpoint/reconcile before copy; copy sidecars only if consistent snapshot guaranteed

option_A_maintenance_window: Stop and verify all writers (writer-quiescence contract).
Checkpoint WAL if authorized; confirm -wal is empty.
Create verified backup (file copy of a quiesced DB).
option_B_online_backup_api: Use SQLite online backup API under an explicitly authorized controlled process with validated source and destination.

blockers: actual_production_schema_not_read: separate authorization required for read-only actual production schema inspection before apply
WAL_OR_SHM_SIDECAR_PRESENT_UNRECONCILED (writer opened DB in WAL mode; database.py sets no journal_mode)

combined_tests.note: Post forward-guard reconciliation (2026-06-14): all combined P271J-L tests pass. The prior 158 passed / 1 failed state and its cause are preserved under forward_guard_reconciliation; the single prior failure was the obsolete P271K p271l file-existence forward guard, now reconciled.

controlled_apply_manifest.executable_only_when: a future prompt contains the exact authorization phrase from authorization_template AND all pre-apply STOP gates pass
controlled_apply_manifest.hash_revalidation_rule: Re-hash the production DB immediately before apply; abort on any drift from the freshly-authorized expected hash.
controlled_apply_manifest.max_execution_boundary: single transaction; CREATE-IF-NOT-EXISTS only; no data backfill; no DROP of legacy objects
controlled_apply_manifest.no_activation_declaration: This apply installs schema only. It MUST NOT insert any activation record, MUST NOT set status to ACTIVE, MUST NOT write any prediction rows. Prospective tables remain empty.

forward_guard_reconciliation.p271l_state_assertion_added: The renamed guard additionally reads the committed P271L artifact and asserts it is preflight-only and NOT applied (preflight_only=true; production_migration_executed / production_schema_modified / deployment_started / prospective_collection_activated / activation_timestamp_inserted / p271m_started / p271n_started all false). Static file read only — no live process, PID, WAL timestamp, network, or production DB connection.
forward_guard_reconciliation.prior_failure_reason: While P271K was the latest authorized phase, its forward guard test_p271l_m_n_not_started forbade scripts/p271l_*.py and tests/test_p271l_*.py. P271L controlled-deployment preflight was then separately authorized and legitimately created those preflight files, so the obsolete p271l token of that guard fired once P271L started. The failure was a stale phase-boundary contract, not a defect in the P271L preflight.

limitations: ACTUAL_PRODUCTION_SCHEMA_NOT_READ_IN_P271L_PREFLIGHT — schema claims are source-grounded only (prospective_capture_ledger.py / database.py), not read from the live DB.
Production DB identity established by raw SHA-256 over bytes only; the DB was never opened via SQLite.

lsof reported no current holders of the production DB at inspection time, but a live backend (PID 1593) plus a recent -shm sidecar indicate an active/potentially-active WAL-mode writer that this read-only task may not quiesce.
Process inventory is a point-in-time snapshot; writer state may change.

maintenance_window_contract.exit_criteria: All writers stopped AND verified AND no DB holders.
states: Announce maintenance window; freeze all scheduled ingest/learning jobs.
Stop backend (lottery_api/app.py) and frontend via stop_all.sh; disable launchd KeepAlive (com.kelvin.lottery.dev.plist) so it does not auto-restart during the window.
Confirm no process holds the production DB (lsof) and PID files are stale/removed.
Reconcile WAL/SHM: ensure WAL is empty and checkpointed; remove or account for any -shm/-wal sidecars before backup.

checks: all expected prospective tables present, exact
prospective tables EMPTY (no rows) unless separately authorized
no runtime integration unless separately approved
backend restart only AFTER verification passes
no activation timestamp / no activation record / no prediction capture

pre_apply_stop_gates: production DB hash drift vs freshly-authorized expected hash
actual production schema not inspected under separate authorization
schema collision or incompatible version detected
production DB already contains unexpected prospective objects
P271N activation accidentally coupled to deployment
process_inventory_note: backend.pid(3418)/frontend.pid(1952) are STALE (not live). A LIVE LotteryNew backend exists out-of-band: PID 1593 (python app.py, cwd lottery_api). The live -shm sidecar (recent mtime) corroborates a recent WAL-mode SQLite connection against the production DB. This live writer cannot be conclusively quiesced in this read-only task.

rollback_plan.authorization_owner: human operator (explicit), same gate as apply
rollback_plan.partial_drop_prohibition: Manual partial DROP of prospective objects is PROHIBITED unless separately designed and authorized.
rollback_plan.post_restore_verification: raw hash == expected_pre_apply_sha256 AND integrity_check == ok
rollback_plan.restore_procedure: restore the verified pre-apply backup over the production DB path (quiesced)
triggers: unexpected legacy row-count or content change
collision or version mismatch discovered during apply
rollback_plan.wal_shm_handling: remove stale -wal/-shm after restore; verify clean state

runtime_db_config.note: lottery_api/database.py:_get_connection sets NO PRAGMA (no journal_mode/WAL, no busy_timeout, no foreign_keys). Default journal mode is rollback (delete). The prospective ledger by contrast sets foreign_keys=ON and busy_timeout=5000 per write.

schema_collision_result.note: Source-level only. ACTUAL production schema is NOT read in P271L. All prospective objects use a 'prospective_' prefix; no legacy table from database.py source shares a name.

writer_inventory.note: Backend (lottery_api/app.py), ingest/backfill HTTP routes, the scheduler/learning integrator, post_draw_pipeline, and ad-hoc upload/controlled-apply scripts can all write the production DB. More than one writer can exist concurrently. database.py sets no busy_timeout (immediate SQLITE_BUSY) and no journal_mode.

writer_quiescence_contract.conservative_rule: If any active or potentially-active writer cannot be conclusively quiesced, the apply manifest MUST be NOT_READY_FOR_APPLY.
evidence_required: no scheduler / post_draw_pipeline / ingest job running
launchd KeepAlive disabled for the window
lsof shows zero holders of lottery_v2.db
